text.title: Vector DB Monitoring — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns in vector DB monitoring setups.

failure-mode.description: No memory metric — DB OOMs without warning; latency spikes minutes before OOM unobserved.
failure-mode.detector: metrics array has no entry with pillar=='memory'

failure-mode.description: Alert without runbook — on-call paged at 03:00 with no idea what to do; resolution time triples.
failure-mode.repair: Author per-alert runbook with at minimum: symptoms, recent-change check, mitigation steps.

failure-mode.description: Single global threshold — same latency cap applied to every workload; alert fires constantly OR never.
failure-mode.detector: monitoring.yaml has one threshold across multiple collections / use cases
failure-mode.repair: Define thresholds per collection / workload class. Use Prometheus labels to scope rules.

failure-mode.description: No capacity trend — disk fills overnight; OOM event surprises team.
failure-mode.repair: Track disk_used_ratio + memory_used_ratio with linear-fit forecast. Alert when forecast OOM <30d.

failure-mode.description: Dashboard without SLO overlay — operators see metric, can't tell if it's OK.
failure-mode.detector: Grafana JSON has timeseries panel without SLO threshold annotation
failure-mode.repair: Add SLO threshold as annotation or step series on every panel.
